chunk_summary: This section focuses on breastfeeding assessment and supplementation decisions in young infants, emphasizing condition-specific counselling and escalation pathways to ensure optimal nutrition and health outcomes.
learning_objectives: Understand the key components of breastfeeding assessment in young infants.
Identify when and how to implement supplementary feeding in breastfed neonates.
Learn escalation pathways for addressing breastfeeding challenges in young infants.
Apply WHO and national guidelines for breastfeeding and supplementation decisions.
key_facts: WHO recommends exclusive breastfeeding for the first 6 months of life to ensure optimal growth and development.
Supplementary feeding should only be considered when medically indicated, such as insufficient milk supply or infant dehydration.
The ABM Clinical Protocol #3 provides detailed guidance on supplementary feeding in healthy term neonates.
The Indian Pediatrics IAP guidelines emphasize the importance of early breastfeeding initiation within the first hour of birth.
The MAA (Mother’s Absolute Affection) program under NHM provides operational guidelines for breastfeeding promotion in India.
reference_quote: "Supplementary feeding should only be introduced when medically indicated, such as in cases of insufficient milk supply or dehydration." (ABM Clinical Protocol #3)
